description: In Java/JVM, validate external input at the deserialization, reflection access, and JNI/native boundaries before internal use.
body: In Java/JVM, parsing, environment, CLI, and network inputs reach the program through deserialization, reflection access, and JNI/native boundaries and must be checked before they are trusted. Use when handling untrusted or external input crossing into Java/JVM code. Do not pass raw external values into internal logic, and avoid assuming well-formed input. Actions: validate at the boundary, reject or normalize malformed values, verify the rejection path with a test, and record the accepted contract. Validation signals include tests on malformed input, a logged/trace rejection, and a defined schema for the boundary. Risk boundary: escalate when input feeds security-sensitive or non-reversible operations; deny by default rather than guessing intent.
